rag_rules: Place input assumptions in component type annexes when bounds are concrete or visible
Use assign only for implementation-level bindings to existing outputs or auxiliary variables
Avoid placing guarantees in implementation scope
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
generic_rules: Use visible identifiers when available to maintain traceability
Avoid assumptions or guarantees that reference subcomponent ports in component type scope
Use named constants or visible relations for bounds in range assumptions
Align helper declaration types with the expression types
Do not encode AADL properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `eq` for equation declarations and `assign` for implementation-level bindings
Use `guarantee` for implementation-level guarantees and `assume` for assumptions
Use `and` for logical conjunctions in range comparisons
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantees in implementation scope
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers
Do not encode AADL properties as AGREE expressions without explicit exposure
